title: How a Russian Propaganda Network Operates – Names, Hierarchies, and Organization
summary: The article reconstructs the Kremlin's chain of command behind the 'Other Ukraine' disinformation project, based on testimony from arrested operative Kirilo Molchanov. It reveals how Putin's Presidential Administration, through intermediaries like Kirienko and the Social Design Agency, coordinates propaganda operations targeting Western support for Ukraine, while also involving FSB and SVR intelligence services.
talking_points: Kirilo Molchanov, a pro-Russian propagandist working for Viktor Medvedchuk's 'Other Ukraine' project, was captured in a Ukrainian sting operation conducted in Poland and transferred to Kyiv

Molchanov revealed a detailed chain of command: Putin delegates to Presidential Administration chief Vaino, who passes instructions to Kirienko, then to Kharichev and Zakharova, who work directly with the Social Design Agency (SDA) on political warfare operations

The project's central objective was undermining Western support for Ukraine by presenting itself as a 'Ukrainian peace alternative' while disseminating pro-Russian narratives adapted for European public debates, especially in Germany and France
Molchanov's FSB contact was Lieutenant General Georgi Grishaev, confirming previous reports about Russian intelligence overseeing foreign interference operations
The case raises unresolved questions about how FSB and SVR intelligence services coordinate with political consulting firms like SDA in running influence operations
concepts: intelligence service coordination with civilian operatives
sting operations and international intelligence cooperation
author: Anton Șehovțov
